title: ORCHESTRATOR.md is a hand-duplicated two-copy surface with no sync mechanism — the declined copy-and-adapt mode, unguarded
evidence: plugins/ca/ORCHESTRATOR.md and plugins/ca-codex/ORCHESTRATOR.md are currently byte-identical (147 lines, `diff` empty), but nothing keeps them so: sync-core.py handles only core/pysrc *.py into hooks/ dirs, and the designed markdown mechanism — core/surface/ templates + tools/build-surface.py (named in ADR-0011's decision and in its own `governs:` line) — does not exist anywhere in the tree (`ls core/surface tools/build-surface.py` → no such file). The second copy was hand-placed.
impact: The governance persona (the plugin's primary behavioral surface) is now in exactly the state ADR-0011's alternatives section rejected: 'two hand-maintained copies ... the exact drift v1 died of, now without even a guard.' Any ca-side ORCHESTRATOR edit that forgets the codex twin ships divergent governance rules to the two hosts, and no gate or CI job notices.
recommendation: Ship the smallest guard now: either extend sync-core.py to vendor ORCHESTRATOR.md (it is currently identical, so byte-sync works today), or add a CI byte-compare step, deferring the full template/build-surface machinery until host-specific divergence is actually needed.
acceptance_criteria: A PR editing one ORCHESTRATOR.md copy without the other fails a mechanical check
The chosen mechanism is reflected in docs/parity.md
